era_context: Same as -2455 and -2456: late Old Kingdom Egypt, Akkadian Empire under Naram-Sin, Harappan civilization in mature phase.

events.title: Harappan trade networks at maximum extent
events.description: The Indus Valley Civilization's trade networks are at their most extensive during the mature Harappan phase. Seals and artifacts from Indus cities appear in Mesopotamian sites (Ur, Lagash, Uruk), the Persian Gulf (Dilmun), and Central Asia. Mesopotamian texts refer to merchants from Meluhha (the Indus Valley). The standardization of weights, measures, and seals suggests a sophisticated commercial system. However, the absence of monumental temples or palaces suggests a different form of political organization than contemporary Egypt or Mesopotamia—possibly merchant-dominated or collective governance.

sources.name: Indus seals found at Mesopotamian sites (Ur, Lagash)
Standardized weights and measures from Harappan sites

events.certainty_note: The existence of Harappan trade networks is confirmed by archaeological evidence. The dating to the mature Harappan phase (c. 2600–1900 BCE) is based on stratigraphic and radiocarbon evidence.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: For -2457, we have virtually no year-specific events. The entry above describes a general feature of the Harappan civilization during this period. The lack of written records from the Indus Valley (the script remains undeciphered) means we cannot reconstruct specific political or military events. Our knowledge is limited to material culture and trade evidence.

graph_edges.note: Harappan trade networks are part of the broader mature Harappan civilization
